bank.dataset_description: UCI Bank Marketing dataset. Classification: predicts whether client subscribes term deposit (y=yes/no). 41K rows, 21 features. 11 categorical columns, most low-cardinality. No missing values. duration is the last call duration — highly predictive but only known after outcome, so excluding it is recommended for realistic prediction. However, it is included in the raw data.

column_semantics.marital: nominal — marital status (married, single, divorced, unknown)
column_semantics.education: ordinal — education level (illiterate < basic.4y < basic.6y < basic.9y < high.school < professional.course < university.degree)
column_semantics.month: nominal — last contact month (no natural cycle order for marketing)
column_semantics.day_of_week: nominal — last contact day (no natural order for marketing)

must_do: encode_categorical_per_column: ordinal for ordered columns, one_hot for nominal
bank.notes: Linear/distance-based model (knn): use one_hot for nominal categoricals to avoid false ordinal assumptions. Use ordinal ONLY for truly ordered columns. MUST clip outliers — linear and distance models are very sensitive to extreme values. Feature selection helps reduce multicollinearity.
